edges.source: Impaired CNP-NPR-B-cGMP Signaling in Growth Plate

edges.target: Impaired CNP-NPR-B-cGMP Signaling in Growth Plate

nodes.description: NPR-B is a transmembrane guanylyl cyclase receptor that converts GTP to cGMP upon binding its ligand CNP. Biallelic loss-of-function mutations in NPR2 eliminate cGMP production in growth plate chondrocytes, disrupting downstream signaling that normally promotes chondrocyte proliferation and hypertrophy.
nodes.id: Impaired CNP-NPR-B-cGMP Signaling in Growth Plate

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the transmembrane natriuretic peptide receptor NPR-B impair skeletal growth and cause acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: We conclude that, although NPR-B is expressed in a number of tissues, its major role is in the regulation of skeletal growth.

evidence.reference_title: The cn/cn dwarf mouse. Histomorphometric, ultrastructural, and radiographic study in mutants corresponding to human acromesomelic dysplasia Maroteaux type (AMDM).
evidence.snippet: The main physeal abnormality was a markedly shortened hypertrophic zone

evidence.reference_title: A novel loss-of-function mutation in Npr2 clarifies primary role in female reproduction and reveals a potential therapy for acromesomelic dysplasia, Maroteaux type.
evidence.snippet: We determined that the loss of Npr2 causes a reduction in the hypertrophic and proliferative zones of the growth plate, but mineralization of skeletal elements is normal.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the transmembrane natriuretic peptide receptor NPR-B impair skeletal growth and cause acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: Three missense mutations were tested in a functional assay and were found to have markedly deficient guanylyl cyclase activity.

evidence.reference_title: A novel NPR2 mutation (p.Arg388Gln) in a patient with acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: Cells expressing HA-R388Q-NPRB showed negligible cGMP responses to C-type natriuretic peptide (CNP) stimulation, indicating that the mutation led to severe loss-of-function.

evidence.reference_title: Novel variants in natriuretic peptide receptor 2 in unrelated patients with acromesomelic dysplasia type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: Several homozygous inactivating mutations in NPR2 have been identified in different AMDM patients. We report five novel variants in affected individuals in four different families.

nodes.description: Impaired chondrocyte hypertrophy and proliferation in the growth plate result in shortened endochondral bones, affecting limbs (particularly middle and distal segments) and vertebral bodies. Normal ultrastructure of individual chondrocytes indicates the defect is in growth plate organization rather than intrinsic cell structure.

evidence.reference_title: The cn/cn dwarf mouse. Histomorphometric, ultrastructural, and radiographic study in mutants corresponding to human acromesomelic dysplasia Maroteaux type (AMDM).
evidence.snippet: Cn/cn mice were two thirds the size of the cn/+.

evidence.reference_title: The cn/cn dwarf mouse. Histomorphometric, ultrastructural, and radiographic study in mutants corresponding to human acromesomelic dysplasia Maroteaux type (AMDM).
evidence.snippet: The disorder causes a shortened physeal hypertrophic zone but normal ultrastructure of cn/cn chondrocytes points to abnormality primarily affecting the hypertrophic zone rather than a structural cell or matrix synthesis problem.
